steps.title: Set up the area equation
steps.content:
The pool has dimensions 21 ft by 13 ft, so its area is $21 \times 13 = 273$ ft².

The concrete path extends $x$ feet on all sides, making the outer rectangle $(21 + 2x)$ ft by $(13 + 2x)$ ft.

The path's area equals the total area minus the pool area:
$$\text{Path area} = (21 + 2x)(13 + 2x) - 273 = 240$$

steps.title: Test choice B using backsolving
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (6):
Total area: $(21 + 12)(13 + 12) = 33 \times 25 = 825$ ft²
Path area: $825 - 273 = 552$ ft²
Fails. Since 552 is far larger than 240, $x = 6$ is too large.

steps.title: Test choice A
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A (3):
Total area: $(21 + 6)(13 + 6) = 27 \times 19 = 513$ ft²
Path area: $513 - 273 = 240$ ft²
That matches choice A — A is correct.
